moduleDescription:
OpenSSL wrapper. Supports OpenSSL >= 1.1.0 dynamically (as default) or statically linked using --dynlibOverride:ssl.

-d:sslVersion=1.2.3 can be used to force an SSL version. This version must be included in the library name. -d:useOpenssl3 may be set for OpenSSL 3 instead.

There is also limited support for OpenSSL 1.0.x which may require -d:openssl10.

Build and test examples:

./bin/nim c -d:ssl -p:. -r tests/stdlib/tssl.nim
./bin/nim c -d:ssl --threads:on -p:. -r tests/stdlib/thttpclient_ssl.nim
./bin/nim c -d:ssl -p:. -r tests/untestable/tssl.nim
./bin/nim c -d:ssl -p:. --dynlibOverride:ssl --passl:-lcrypto --passl:-lssl -r tests/untestable/tssl.nim
./bin/nim r --putenv:NIM_TESTAMENT_REMOTE_NETWORKING:1 -d:ssl -p:testament/lib --threads:on tests/untestable/thttpclient_ssl_remotenetwork.nim

entries.name: useOpenssl3
entries.code: useOpenssl3 {.booldefine.} = false

entries.code: DLLSSLName = "(libssl-1_1-x64|ssleay64|libssl64).dll"

entries.code:
proc SSL_set_tlsext_host_name(ssl: SslPtr; name: cstring): int {.raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Set the SNI server name extension to be used in a client hello. Returns 1 if SNI was set, 0 if current SSL configuration doesn't support SNI.

entries.name: SSL_get_servername
entries.code:
proc SSL_get_servername(ssl: SslPtr; typ: cint = TLSEXT_NAMETYPE_host_name): cstring {.
    cdecl, dynlib: DLLSSLName, importc, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Retrieve the server name requested in the client hello. This can be used in the callback set in SSL_CTX_set_tlsext_servername_callback to implement virtual hosting. May return nil.

entries.name: SSL_CTX_set_tlsext_servername_callback

arguments.name: cb
arguments.type: proc (ssl: SslPtr; cb_id: int; arg: pointer): int {.cdecl.}

entries.description:
Set the callback to be used on listening SSL connections when the client hello is received.

The callback should return one of:

  • SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_OK
  • SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_ALERT_WARNING
  • SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_ALERT_FATAL
  • SSL_TLSEXT_ERR_NOACK

entries.name: SSL_CTX_set_tlsext_servername_arg

    raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Set the pointer to be used in the callback registered to SSL_CTX_set_tlsext_servername_callback.
